carry_forwards: GitHub Issues backing is the deferred real-service follow-on; it now only needs to implement TrackingStore and join the BACKINGS contract matrix.
F4 decided: no Repository rename - TrackingStore is the contract, Repository is the resolver/policy layer. Revisit only if a Review/Practice store makes the policy/storage split need renaming.
Repository still resolves .lume/ from the filesystem (find_lume_dir) even under a non-fs backing; a fully remote backing would need workstream discovery to move behind the contract too.
The other two scope.md contracts (Review, Practice) remain single-implementation; the same discovery-first 'find where the boundary really is' approach applies when they get a second member.

done_when.clause: A second Tracking backing exists and is exercised by lume's verbs (resolve/list, load+save state, lifecycle transitions)
done_when.evidence: SQLiteStore (store.py) implements TrackingStore; the full lifecycle (new/open/approve/start/handback/accept + second iteration + decide/retro/close) runs against it in tests AND live via the CLI - the workstream persists entirely in lume.db, no fs files. A third backing (InMemoryStore) also passes the shared contract matrix.

done_when.clause: The backing is selected through a documented contract rather than by editing engine code
done_when.evidence: LUME_BACKING env (fs default, sqlite) is read once at the cli edge and injected into Repository; no engine code below branches on backing. Unknown value errors clearly.

done_when.clause: lume's existing behaviour on the filesystem backing is unchanged (suite green)
done_when.evidence: Filesystem is the default; on-disk layout and behaviour identical. 267 tests pass, including the full pre-existing suite; E1-E2 were behaviour-preserving and grep-verified.

done_when.clause: Any contract leaks surfaced are resolved or recorded as decisions
done_when.evidence: Discovery's leak audit drove E2 (Workstream's direct I/O moved behind the store; cli ws._path leak fixed) and E3 (state.py validation decoupled from path I/O). Migrate.py is recorded (F6) as a deliberate local-only bridge, excluded from the live contract.

overall_verdict: Net positive. The Tracking contract is now real: persistence lives behind an injected TrackingStore, lume runs unchanged on the filesystem and equally on SQLite, a backing is chosen by config not code, and a cross-backing matrix enforces the contract. Discovery's reframing (move the boundary below Workstream) was the decisive call - it turned a would-be shallow Repository subclass into a clean seam. The ceremony (a ~40-line protocol + two real implementations + a test double + the matrix) bought back exactly what scope.md's design test asks for: the second member of the example pair expressed cleanly, now proven by lived use rather than design intent. Honest scope: GitHub Issues (the named real-service pair member) is deferred to a future workstream; this one proved replaceability with SQLite, the cheapest backing that breaks every filesystem assumption.

stage_verdicts.cost: One analysis iteration, no engine code (plus a one-line skeleton fix).
stage_verdicts.saved: Found the load-bearing truth before any code: the Tracking contract was split (Repository) and mislocated (Workstream did its own file I/O, even state.json on the write path). That reframed the work from 'subclass Repository' to 'move the boundary below Workstream' - without it, E1/E2 would have built a backing that intercepted almost nothing.

Resolved all seven forks with rationale and sized the work into reviewable slices (E1 protocol, E2 relocation, E3 second backing, E4 matrix, E5 closeout). The TrackingStore interface sketch gave execution a concrete target. Folding the two CLI bugs into E2 kept scope honest.

stage_verdicts.cost: Four iterations: protocol+FilesystemStore, the Workstream relocation (the big one), SQLite backing + selection, in-memory double + contract matrix.
stage_verdicts.saved: Each slice stayed behaviour-preserving and grep-verified. The relocation was mechanical once the contract existed; the second backing then dropped in with ~60 lines and no change above the seam. The contract matrix turned 'backing-agnostic' into an enforced check across three backings.
Honest done-when assessment + the naming decision recorded; closes the workstream on its own verbs.
